items.title: Page updated: Knox County Probate Court

page_lines: handles all estates, both formal and informal; guardianships for both adults and minors; conservatorships, changes of name; adoptions; and other legal matters. Knox County Probate Office holds records reaching back to 1860, with anything prior filed in either Waldo or Lincoln Counties.
Probate Court is held on the first and third Wednesday of each month, and at other times as necessary. Both the Judge of Probate and Register of Probate are elected positions.
9:00 a.m. - 3:00 p.m., Monday, Tuesday, Thursday & Friday excluding holidays or otherwise posted
What do you need to apply for a passport?
: Do not sign the form as it must be signed in front of a Passport Agent.
Passport application forms are available FREE of charge at
* Proof of U.S. Citizenship: state-issued birth certificate or previous U.S. passport
* Proof of Identity: current valid photo ID (military, state, or driver’s license). If your photo ID is from a different state than the state in which you are applying, bring a second photo ID.
* Passport photo: You must provide a recent photo with your application. We can take and print a photo for you if you do not already have one.
If the passport is for a child, BOTH parents must be present unless there is a notarized affidavit (Form DS-3053) attached with a copy of the identification used for the notary.
* Passport Book: $130 for adults 16 and over, $100 for children under 16
* Passport Card: $30 for adults 16 and over, $15 for children under 16
* Passport Book/Card: Check or money order to US DEPT OF STATE
* Processing Fee: Cash, Check, or Debit/Credit Card
* Vital Records - request birth, death or marriage records through the Maine State Office of
(207) 287-3771 or the town or city clerk’s office holding the vital records you need.
